title: Crisp Cooling Jamaican Saltfish Hash
description: A briny and crisp Jamaican dish featuring saltfish, perfect for a hangover cure, made with light brown soft sugar and kombu-cold-extract, and cooked on a comal.
instructions: Rinse the saltfish and soak it in water for 30 minutes, then drain and flake it into small pieces.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil on a comal over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, bell pepper, and scotch bonnet pepper, and cook until the vegetables are softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the flaked saltfish, light brown soft sugar, and kombu-cold-extract, and stir to combine.
Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is lightly browned and crispy.
Use a spatula to press the mixture down onto the comal and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Flip the hash over and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until crispy and golden brown.
Remove from heat and serve hot.
